title: Juno: New Origins
anchor_summary: Physics-heavy aerospace construction sandbox centered on designing custom rockets, planes, and vehicles, then testing them in realistic flight/orbital simulation with contracts, progression, and automation tools.

similar_games.title: Kerbal Space Program
similar_games.why_similar: The clearest match: build spacecraft from parts, manage staging and orbital mechanics, run missions, and iterate through simulation-driven launches.

similar_games.title: SimplePlanes
similar_games.why_similar: Shares the same build-and-test philosophy, highly customizable vehicle construction, and physics-based flight sandbox, with more focus on aircraft than spaceflight.

similar_games.title: Kerbal Space Program 2
similar_games.why_similar: Another part-based space program sandbox built around rocket design, launch execution, orbital planning, and engineering experimentation.

similar_games.title: Trailmakers
similar_games.why_similar: Vehicle construction sandbox where players assemble and test custom machines across different traversal types, though it is less simulation-authentic than Juno.

similar_games.title: Besiege
similar_games.why_similar: Engineering sandbox focused on designing contraptions and refining them through physics-based testing, echoing Juno’s iterative build-simulate loop.

llm_taxonomy.evidence_summary: Stored descriptions consistently present Juno: New Origins as a 3D aerospace sandbox centered on designing, building, programming, testing, and iterating vehicles with realistic physics and orbital mechanics. The core loop is construction plus simulation rather than combat or authored story: customizable parts, engine tuning, drag/lift/orbit simulation, map planning, fuel management, persistent sandbox, planet creation tools, and community-shared crafts all strongly fit engineering_sandbox_sim. Career-mode contracts, milestones, and a tech tree add progression, but they do not override the primary identity of a physics-heavy vehicle engineering sandbox.
